From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1997 single by Jay-Z

| "Who You Wit" | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") by [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") | |
| from the album  *[Sprung (Music from and Inspired by the Motion Picture)](/wiki/Sprung_(soundtrack) "Sprung (soundtrack)") & [In My Lifetime, Vol. 1](/wiki/In_My_Lifetime,_Vol._1 "In My Lifetime, Vol. 1")* | |
| Released | May 20, 1997 |
| Recorded | 1996 |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [East Coast hip hop](/wiki/East_Coast_hip_hop "East Coast hip hop") |
| Length | 4:07 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | * [Qwest](/wiki/Qwest_Records "Qwest Records") * [Warner Bros.](/wiki/Warner_Bros._Records "Warner Bros. Records") |
| [Songwriters](/wiki/Songwriter "Songwriter") | * [Shawn Carter](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") * [David Willis](/wiki/Ski_Beatz "Ski Beatz") * [Jeff Lorber](/wiki/Jeff_Lorber "Jeff Lorber") |

| [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") singles chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | "[Feelin' It](/wiki/Feelin%27_It_(Jay-Z_song) "Feelin' It (Jay-Z song)")"  (1997) | "**Who You Wit**"  (1997) | "[(Always Be My) Sunshine](/wiki/(Always_Be_My)_Sunshine "(Always Be My) Sunshine")"  (1997) | | |
|  | |
| Music video | |

"**Who You Wit**" is a 1997 single by [rapper](/wiki/Rapping "Rapping") [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") that is produced by [Ski](/wiki/Ski_(producer) "Ski (producer)"). It was released in promotion of the 1997 comedy film *[Sprung](/wiki/Sprung_(film) "Sprung (film)")* and appears on its soundtrack, *[Sprung (Music from and Inspired by the Motion Picture)](/wiki/Sprung_(soundtrack) "Sprung (soundtrack)")*. Its beat samples "Night Love" by the [Jeff Lorber Fusion](/wiki/Jeff_Lorber#The_Jeff_Lorber_Fusion_(1977-1981) "Jeff Lorber").

A track named "**Who You Wit II**" appears on Jay-Z's second album *[In My Lifetime, Vol. 1](/wiki/In_My_Lifetime,_Vol._1 "In My Lifetime, Vol. 1")*. It features the same beat, but adds an additional verse that the original version lacked.
